name: Whole Orange and Almond Cake and Marmalade Cheesecake Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F/180°C/Gas 4 and line two 1kg/2lb loaf tins with greaseproof paper.
2. Cook the oranges: Cover with cold water and bring to a simmer. Cook for about 1 hour (skin on) until very tender. Remove from the pan using a slotted spoon and allow any excess water to drain off. Cut into quarters.
3. Whizz all of the orange quarters, including pith and rind, to a purée in a food processor or blender. Leave to cool.
4. To make the sponge: Mix together the ground almonds, caster sugar, and baking powder. Whisk the eggs until the whisk leaves a trail when lifted out of the mixture, then fold in the almond mixture and orange purée.
5. Divide the mixture between the tins and bake for about 45 minutes. The cake should be firm to the touch and if pierced with a knife, the blade should come out clean. Cut into slices and serve decorated with mint sprigs.
6. Accompany with a good dollop of marmalade cheesecake cream.
MARMALADE CHEESECAKE CREAM:
1. Beat together the cream cheese and caster sugar until the sugar has dissolved and creamed.
2. Add the orange marmalade (full 225g for this quantity to produce a strong orange flavor) - mix completely.
3. Lightly fold in the whipped cream. Spoon into a bowl, cover, and chill until firm.
4. Spoon on cake plates using a warmed tablespoon to create a decorative swirl. Alternatively, serve the cream in a separate dish.
